summary_text: 入れるなら、`x-auto` の本体ルールは [docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md) に寄せて、[skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md) は薄く参照だけにするのが筋です。トーンは「常体ベース、ただし断定を少しだけ和らげる」がちょうど中間です。以下の文言なら、そのまま skill に入れられます。 ```md ## Japanese Tone Control Japanese microcopy / social copy should aim for a middle register: not overly assertive, not overly polite, and not academic. - Base voice: plain form (常体) as the default. - Preferred sentence endings: - factual / observed: `〜だ`, `〜する`, `〜になる`, `〜といえる` - moderate softening: `〜かもしれない`, `〜だろう`, `〜と見てよい`, `〜になりそうだ` - reader-facing closing lines only: `〜です`, `〜ます`, `〜ですね` - Use `です/ます` sparingly. Do not make every sentence polite. - Keep one clear claim per paragraph. Do not stack assertions, intensifiers, and hedges in the same sentence. - Use hedge language only where certainty is not justified by the source or context. - When the source is strong, soften the framing of the conclusion, not the evidence. Example: `資料からは〜と読める` rather than `絶対に〜だ`. - Avoid tone that sounds either preachy or apologetic. The copy should sound informed, calm, and slightly conversational. Prohibited extremes: - Over-assertive: `絶対`, `必ず`, `明らかに`, `完全に`, `〜しかない`, `断言できる`, `〜に違いない` - Over-polite / weak: `〜と思います` の連発, `〜かもしれません` の連発, `恐縮ですが`, `〜いただけますと幸いです`, `でございます` - Do not combine two extremes in one line, such as `絶対に〜かもしれない`. Balance rule: - Use one hedge at most per sentence when uncertainty exists. - Use at most one polite-ending sentence per 3-4 sentences unless the copy is support-oriented. - Prefer softeners like `〜と見られる`, `〜になりそうだ`, `〜と考える` over timid filler. ``` Before/after examples: - `これは絶対に失敗しません。` -> `これは、かなり失敗しにくい設計です。` - `今すぐやるべきです。` -> `今のうちに整えておくのがよさそうです。` - `この方法が唯一の正解です。` -> `この方法は、現時点ではいちばん扱いやすい選択です。` If you want, I can patch the contract and the adapter with this wording next.
